quotes.text: I spent a whole afternoon once trying to explain to my mother what I actually do. Not the cooking, the other part — the production, the sourcing. And she kept nodding and then at the end she said 'so you go to the market' and I said yes, Mom, essentially I go to the market.
quotes.speaker: José Andrés
quotes.context: Keynote conversation at the Madrid Fusión culinary summit, January 2020
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Andrés consistently uses warm, funny family anecdotes to puncture the pretension of haute cuisine discourse, and the immigrant-parent dynamic here feels authentic to his public storytelling. The subtle tell is that the punchline is a little too perfectly circular — real anecdotes rarely resolve quite this cleanly.

quotes.text: People ask me what I listen to before a match and I always disappoint them. It's not some big pump-up playlist. Honestly, lately it's been a podcast about the Roman aqueduct system. I find it genuinely calming.
quotes.speaker: Serena Williams
quotes.context: Interview with Gayle King on CBS Mornings, February 2023
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Serena has spoken often about her eclectic intellectual interests and deliberately deflating the 'athlete mystique,' so the self-aware disappointment of the answer feels completely her. The subtle giveaway is the oddly specific 'Roman aqueduct system,' which is just a touch too particular to be real.

quotes.text: You love to hate and trash me
quotes.speaker: Uwe Boll
quotes.context: Filmmaker responding to critics of his work on the Armie Hammer movie 'Citizen Vigilante'

source.title: Entertainment — Uwe Boll Rips His Critics While Defending Armie Hammer Movie: “Open Your Eyes”

quotes.text: I'm tired of hearing about money, money, money, money, money. I just want to play the game, drink Pepsi, wear Reebok.
quotes.speaker: Shaquille O'Neal
quotes.context: NBA center, in July 1996 after leaving the Orlando Magic for the Lakers, when asked about contract money

source.title: 'I Just Want To Play The Game, Drink Pepsi, Wear Reebok': Remembering The Time Shaquille O'Neal Dropped Random Knowledge

quotes.text: You shouldn't let poets lie to you
quotes.speaker: Björk
quotes.context: In a 1988 TV interview, after taking apart a television to explain how it works and recounting how an Icelandic poet's spooky description had once scared her.

source.title: Commentary on Björk's 1988 "Wonder of Television" interview

quotes.text: Someone sent me a review once that said my technique was 'technically proficient but emotionally opaque' and I've been thinking about that for about six years. Not because I disagree. I just keep wondering what the opposite would actually sound like.
quotes.speaker: Yo-Yo Ma
quotes.context: Feature profile in The New Yorker, 'The Instrument,' published April 2019
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Yo-Yo Ma is known for deeply philosophical, slightly meandering self-reflection on performance and meaning, and returning to a critic's phrase for years is entirely consistent with his intellectual character. The giveaway is the wry final line, which is a hair too aphorism-adjacent for his usual rambling thoughtfulness.
